entry.title: Re: How to trigger automatic email notifications to custo...
entry.content:
Hi @Chetan_attal ,

Short answer: There is no standard, out-of-the-box feature in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition that sends a payment-confirmation email to the customer when an incoming payment is posted, and Output Management cannot do it either. The supported way to build this is event-driven: consume the Journal Entry business event and send the email from SAP Integration Suite.

1. Is there a standard, out-of-the-box configuration to email the customer when a payment is posted?

No. Incoming payments (on-account, advance/down payment, or invoice clearing) all post as accounting/journal entry documents, and there is no standard app or configuration that generates an outbound customer email on that posting. The standard payment-communication objects in the product — payment advices and payment media — belong to outgoing Accounts Payable payments to suppliers, not to incoming customer payments, so they don't cover your requirement. Reference: Feature Scope Description 2602.

2. Can this be achieved with SAP S/4HANA Output Management (via the payment/clearing document)?

No. Output Control in Public Cloud supports the channels PRINT, EMAIL and EDI, but only for the business objects that are output-enabled (e.g. billing documents/customer invoices, sales orders, purchase orders, outbound deliveries, dunning). The FI journal entry / clearing document produced by an incoming payment is not an output-management-enabled object, so there is no output type or determination you can configure against it. Reference: Output Parameter Determination, Working with Output Items.

3. Recommended workaround

Recommended: Business Events + SAP Integration Suite. This is the clean, upgrade-safe pattern in Public Cloud. Note it requires significant effort to be established.

Publish the event: every posted payment creates a Journal Entry, which raises a standard Journal Entry business event. You expose it through Enterprise Event Enablement — set up a Communication Arrangement for scenario SAP_COM_0092 and create the outbound topic binding in Enterprise Event Enablement – Configure Channel Binding. Reference: Business Event Handling, Journal Entry events on Business Accelerator Hub.

Consume and send: route the event to SAP Integration Suite (Cloud Integration). Note the messaging layer options — the standalone SAP Event Mesh is being phased out for new (BTPEA) contracts; use Event Mesh for Integration Suite (EMIS), which is included, or Advanced Event Mesh if you need higher scale. Reference: Advanced Event Mesh.

Important: the business event payload delivers only document keys (company code, fiscal year, accounting document), not the amount, currency, or customer email. Your iFlow therefore needs to (a) call the Journal Entry API to read amount + currency, (b) read the customer's email from Business Partner master data, then (c) send the mail via the Mail adapter with your dynamic text.

Filter: a Journal Entry event fires for all FI postings, so filter in the iFlow (e.g. by document/transaction type or clearing indicator) to isolate genuine incoming customer payments and avoid emailing on unrelated postings.

Other suggested options from your text:

  • On a custom BAdI: possible but not advisable here. Released FI posting BAdIs run in the ABAP Cloud sandbox and are not intended to fire external customer emails synchronously during posting; keeping the logic in Integration Suite is cleaner, decoupled, and easier to maintain.
  • On Situation Handling: not suitable, because it notifies internal SAP business users only and cannot target external, non-SAP customers.

Summary

No standard feature and no Output Management route exists for this. Build it event-driven: publish the Journal Entry event via Enterprise Event Enablement (SAP_COM_0092), consume it in SAP Integration Suite (EMIS or Advanced Event Mesh), enrich the amount/currency and the customer email via APIs, filter for real incoming payments, and send through the Mail adapter. Confirm the exact event payload on the SAP Business Accelerator Hub for release 2602 before you design the iFlow.

Hope it helps.
Best regards,
Michal

I understand your could not generate maintenance key for S/4HANA system 850450600.

I can see you have a valid SAP Rise contract; however, the blocker is that there is no active maintenance contract for installation 21167223. It also appears that maintenance coverage for all your on-premise installations has expired. 

Regarding this issue, you may refer to KBA 2436412 - License Keys application shows message: You do not have a maintenance contract for this installation - SAP for Me

To move this forward, please contact your SAP Account Manager or SAP Order Management to review and update the maintenance coverage on your account:

My product support team can only tell you if you could select the related license material to generate or renew the key, we can't fix issues like this. Therefore, please work directly with SAP Order Management (contracts) and/or your SAP Account Manager to check your account and make any necessary updates. After your account is updated and authorized for the product, you will be able to use it.

If you still have such issue after contacting with them, please do not hesitate to contact with us again.

Thanks for your understanding and cooperation.

entry.title: Clearing G/L Line Items Using Processing Rules in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
entry.content:
In today's fast-paced business environment, efficiency and accuracy in financial management are paramount. SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition offers powerful automated processing rules that simplify and expedite the bank statement reconciliation process, ensuring financial data is accurate and up to date. This blog walks you through how to automatically clear G/L line items in payment documents against bank items using Processing Rules - a capability that significantly reduces manual effort and minimizes the risk of human error.

Note: All images and data referenced in this blog are from SAP internal systems, sample data, or demo systems. Any resemblance to real data is purely coincidental.

Finance teams often face the challenge of manually matching and clearing G/L line items in payment documents against corresponding bank items. Without automation, this process is time-consuming and error-prone, especially in high-volume transaction environments. The key question is: How can G/L line items in a payment document be automatically cleared against the bank item using Processing Rules in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition?

When bank statements are imported, the system initially attempts to match open items using configured posting rules and interpretation algorithms. However, if the system fails to match an incoming payment with an open item using predefined posting rules, the remaining bank statement information is checked against custom processing rules. 

Solution: Step-by-Step Configuration Guide
Understanding the Two Core Concepts Before You Begin

Before diving into the steps, it is important to understand the distinction between Posting Rules and Processing Rules in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition:

Posting Rules are predefined and preconfigured by SAP and cannot be altered by customers. They are standardized to ensure consistency and compliance.
Processing Rules are fully customizable by customers to meet their specific business needs. This distinction allows for a balance between maintaining system integrity and providing customization options.

Step 1: Open the Manage Processing Rules App

  • Log into your SAP Fiori Launchpad and search for the Manage Processing Rules – For Bank Statements app (also referred to as Manage Bank Statement Reprocessing Rules).
  • This app is where you define the conditions and actions that processing rules will perform on bank statement items.

Step 2: Create a New Processing Rule

  • Click the Create button to open the rule editor.
  • On the Processing Rule tab, fill in the following header information:
    • Description: Enter a clear, descriptive name for the rule (e.g., "Auto-Clear G/L Items – Incoming Payments").
    • Journal Entry Type: Select the appropriate document type (e.g., DZ – Customer Payment).
    • Rule for: Select Incoming Payments or Outgoing Payments as applicable.
    • Action Type: Choose the relevant action type. For clearing G/L line items, select Clear in Sequence – Activates interpretation and allocation algorithms to clear open items.

Step 3: Define Conditions

  • Navigate to the Conditions tab. This is where the rule "matches" the correct bank statement items. [4]
  • Add one or more conditions using the following attributes:
    • Attributes: Categories of data to filter on - examples include Amount, Company Code, Currency, External Transaction, House Bank, Memo Line, Partner IBAN, Partner Name, etc.
    • Operators: Define the type of comparison - examples include Equals, Contains, Between, Greater Than, etc.
    • From/To: Define the value to search for. 
  • Ensure conditions are specific enough so the rule only triggers for the right transactions. It is also recommended to include House Bank and Account ID conditions to restrict the rule to a specific bank account.

Step 4: Configure the Action (Based on Action Type)

Depending on the Action Type selected in Step 2, configure the corresponding action details: 

  • For Clear in Sequence: Choose the Processing Instruction MTCH that dictate how the system locates the open items.
     
    • Configure the Processing Instruction to define how the system searches memo line data against open items.
    • Select the Source Field from the dropdown list. The Source Field field refers to a field in the payment advice item or bank statement item (and also in the bank statement item's subitem), for example, the Journal Entry (Advice) or the Assignment Reference (Bank Statement) field. By determining the source field, you tell the system to use the value in that specific field of the payment advice item or bank statement item, and to look for a matching value in the field of an open item.
    • Select the Use Source Field As from the dropdown list. The Use Source Field as field refers to a field of an open item, for example, the Journal Entry (BELNR), the Reference (XBLNR), or Reference Key (XREF) field of open items. By determining this field, you tell the system to look for matching values in this field.



Note: If you select the Match Date checkbox, the system also compares the dates of the payment advice item and of the open items. If you deselect the Match Date checkbox, the system does not compare any dates, and simply uses the values defined in the Source Field and Use Source Field as fields in order to match items.

Restriction
If you select bank-statement-related fields as the source field in the sequence, you cannot select the Match Date checkbox. You can only select the Match Date checkbox with payment-advice-related fields.

If you select the Intercompany Open Items checkbox, the system also includes intercompany open items in the search for matching open items with the defined values for the Source Field and Use Source Field as fields. If you deselect the Intercompany Open Items checkbox, the system does not include any intercompany open items, and only searches for open items belonging to the company code of the bank statement item.

The sequence that you define here is submitted to the allocation algorithm that is determined in the selected processing instruction. The allocation algorithm then uses this sequence to compare the values in specific fields in payment advice items or bank statement items and open items, and thus, to identify matching items which can then be cleared.

Example

In the Clear in Sequence section, you have selected a processing instruction that contains an allocation algorithm with the correct configurations for payment advice processing, such as the MTCH processing instruction. In the Match Items in Sequence section, you define the following values:


Row Number

Source Field

Use Source Field as

1

Memo Line (Bank Statement Item)

Journal Entry

2

Customer Reference Number (Bank Statement Item)

Reference

The system reads this sequence as follows: First, take the value in the Memo Line field of the bank statement item, and compare it with the value in the Journal Entry field of the open items. If the values match, the bank statement item and open item are matched. If not, proceed with the second row: Take the value in the Customer Reference Number (Bank Statement Item) field of the bank statement item, and compare it with the value in the Reference field of the open items. If the values match, the bank statement item and open item are matched. If not, continue with the next processing instruction, or display the item in the Reprocess Bank Statement Items app for manual processing.

  • Under Open Item type section, select G/L Items. The default value of this field is AP/AR Items. If you want to, you can determine that you want the processing instruction to look for G/L items. If you do select G/L Items, you need to specify a range of G/L accounts that the processing instruction should search to find matching open G/L items to be cleared.
  • Define residual item handling, reason codes, or on-account fallback options as needed. 
  • Click on Apply.

Step 5: Save the Rule

  • Click Create to save the rule. 
  • The rule is now saved and available for manual use in the Reprocess Bank Statement Items app.

Step 6: Automate the Rule

To have the rule apply automatically during bank statement import - without manual intervention - you must activate automation: 

  • Select the saved rule in the list view.
  • Click the Automate Rule button (available in the top-right or via the action menu).
  • In the pop-up dialog, select the Procedure Type:
    • 001 – One-Step Posting: Only Posting Area 1 (PA1 – Bank Accounting) will be posted. The Bank Accounting journal entry is posted to the G/L account mentioned in the processing rule and the main bank G/L account. 
    • 002 – Two-Step Posting: Both PA1 (Bank Accounting) and PA2 (Subledger Accounting) will be posted. The Bank Accounting journal entry posts to the main bank G/L account and the bank subaccount; the Subledger Accounting journal entry posts to the bank subaccount and the AP/AR account.
  • Confirm by clicking Automate Rule.

The rule is now active and will be applied automatically for the next incoming bank statement imports.

Step 7: Verify Processing via the Reprocess Bank Statement Items App

After the next bank statement is imported, verify that the rule was applied correctly:

  • Open the Reprocess Bank Statement Items app.
  • Locate the relevant bank statement item and check the processing log.
  • The log will confirm whether the rule was applied and the journal entries were posted successfully.
  • If no open item is found for a bank statement item, the item will need to be handled manually in this app.

Important Note: Handling Multiple Matching Rules

If multiple processing rules match the same bank statement item, the system stops the application of the processing rule and raises the following error:

"Bnk stmnt X itm Y matches conditions of several rules. No rule applied."

In this case, the normal posting rule assigned to the external transaction code in the SSCUI will be applied instead. To avoid this, ensure your rule conditions are specific and non-overlapping. 

Conclusion

Automating the clearing of G/L line items using Processing Rules in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition is a highly effective way to improve efficiency and accuracy in financial close and bank reconciliation processes. By configuring the Manage Processing Rules – For Bank Statements app with the right conditions, action types, and procedure types, finance teams can significantly reduce manual workload and ensure consistent, accurate postings. [1]

Key takeaways:

  • Posting Rules are SAP-delivered and fixed; Processing Rules are customer-configurable.
  • Use Action Type: Clear in Sequence with the processing instruction MTCH to automatically clear G/L open items.
  • Always activate automation on the rule to ensure it runs during bank statement import.
  • Monitor results via the Reprocess Bank Statement Items app and handle exceptions manually where needed. 

For further reading, refer to the SAP S/4HANA Cloud documentation on Automatic Bank Statement Processing and KBA 3780076 on SAP for Me.

entry.title: Re: Reject Creating Outbound Delivery
entry.content:
Hello @mrbeam 

What is the business rationale behind that requirement? Why would you want to prevent delivery creation in EWM?

One very dirty method could be to throw an abort message / short dump in the inbound mapping BAdI. But again why?

Such rejections are surefire recipe for a disaster, for ERP-EWM inconsistencies - deliveries created in ERP will never be created in EWM; they will just stick forgotten for ever in qRFC queues. Typically EWM implementation struggle with the opposite challenge - to keep ERP and EWM consistent and minimize outstanding qRFC entries.

Best regards

Dominik Tylczynski

entry.title: Re: Too many line items in the Material document

It is better to split the documents rather than change the length of the ZEILE field.

  • SAP should continue to report the error even if you change the length of the ZEILE field.
  • Impact Analysis should be performed
  • Also keep in mind that material documents can generate financial and controling documents.

entry.title: Re: Screen Painter Blank in SAPMV45A screen
entry.content:
The note 2297134 is not about "blank dynpro", it's about regenerating the "dynpro load" ; your issue is about the "dynpro source".

See in the Version Management why and who cleared the dynpro layout, and see in the transport request if other objects are impacted.

Solution : restore the previous version or import the dynpro from another system via the Transport Management System.

Contact SAP Support if you need more assistance.

entry.title: Re: Unable to Set Payment Terms and Payment Method for Ve...
entry.content:
Dear @StrOk,

The posted result is standard system behavior. In the journal entry the residual supplier item has Is Negative Posting = Yes and Debit/Credit Code = S, confirming that it was created as a negative debit posting. Although displayed as a negative amount in the Debit column, its balance effect corresponds to a credit posting

There is no residual-item-level option in Clear Outgoing Payments (F1367) to force the item to be posted as a positive amount in the Credit column. The residual amount should retain the same sign as the underlying supplier item and should not be reversed only to change its presentation

Negative postings are controlled more broadly at company-code and document-type level. Changing this configuration would affect other postings and is not recommended solely to change the display of this residual item. No correction is required for the posted item

More details below:

SAP Help – Negative Postings

SAP KBA 3157729 – General Journal Entries Negative Postings SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

Best Regards,

Dawid

>Also noticed a minor bug, when you enter text and resize the text is gone in the same field.

Frank
